formatted_address: 21106 Kenswick Meadows Ct, Humble, TX 77338, USA
pitch_rationale: Area-weighted across 8 roof segments from Google Solar API (imageryQuality=HIGH): 30.86° → 7:12. Solar's underlying DSM model reports ~5° MAE in published benchmarks (Satellite Sunroof, NeurIPS 2024).
footprint_rationale: The subject home is identified by the orange dashed bounding box and reticle. The box spans approximately 261 × 272 pixels (55.3 ft × 57.7 ft). The visible roof is a hip-style structure with lighter-colored (newer) shingles, consistent with a recently completed build. Construction lumber is visible around the property perimeter, partially obscuring roof edges. The actual hip roof polygon was traced at approximately 210 × 200 pixels (44.5 × 42.4 ft), yielding a plan-view footprint of ~1,895 sq ft with a 92% hip-fill factor applied to the measured rectangle. Cross-check using the bounding box at ~60% fill gives ~1,913 sq ft — agreement within ~1%. No valleys or rake edges present on this simple hip roof. Ridge estimated at 85 px (18 ft). Four hip lines from ridge ends to corners measured at ~118 px each in plan (≈25 ft each, 100 ft total). Eave runs the full perimeter: 2×(44.5+42.4) = 174 ft. No clearly attached garage or addition is visible as a separate roofed mass within the orange box boundary. Confidence is moderate-low due to construction activity obscuring portions of the roof outline.

findings.description: Multiple small dark objects (at least 4–5 discrete spots) scattered across the flat/low-slope roof surface. While some are consistent with standard roof penetrations or vent boots, several are irregularly distributed and do not align with a typical vent pattern, indicating loose items or debris on the membrane.
findings.location_description: Central flat roof section, distributed across the full deck within the orange bounding box

findings.description: Roof surface is a bright light gray/white — distinctly different from every surrounding neighbor home, which all show dark gray asphalt shingles. This is consistent with a TPO, EPDM, or coated flat membrane roof, which carries its own maintenance and inspection considerations compared to a pitched shingle roof.
findings.location_description: Entire visible flat roof deck within the orange bounding box

condition.rationale: The subject roof within the orange bounding box is a flat or very low-slope section covered in a light-colored membrane (white/light gray), which stands in clear contrast to all adjacent neighboring homes that have dark gray pitched shingle roofs. The surface is largely uniform with no visible tarps, large discoloration patches, structural sag along the roofline, or moss growth. However, several small dark objects are scattered across the membrane deck that do not conform to a standard vent/penetration layout, warranting a closer ground-level look to confirm whether these are equipment, debris, or damage indicators. Notably, construction lumber and materials are visible stacked in the yard immediately adjacent to the home on the east and south sides, suggesting active or recent renovation work that may be relevant to the roof's current state. No missing material, structural deformation, or tarping is visible from this altitude and angle.
